verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type 1 single-pass transmembrane protein that functions as an ER Ca2+ sensor. Upon store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-PM junctions where its cytosolic SOAR/CAD domain directly gates Orai channels at the plasma membrane. Critically, STIM1 itself has a short N-terminal extracellular (ER-luminal) domain exposed on the outer leaflet of the ER, and when it traffics to the plasma membrane (a documented minority pool confirmed by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry), its N-terminal domain is extracellular. This stable PM-resident pool with an extracellular domain makes STIM1 accessible from the cell exterior.
